author: Nhat Nguyen
message:
Push down TopN into count aggregation (#153615)

For queries like

```
FROM .. | STATS c=COUNT(*), ... BY k1, k2... | SORT c | LIMIT N
```

We can push down the TopN into the aggregation to avoid emitting a large 
number of keys and aggregated values. This skips the costly emit phase
for large keys/values and the TopN time - even though TopN itself has
been sped up significantly with ParallelTopN.

To keep the PR small, only the count aggregation supports this pushdown for now.

author: Jack Conradson
message:
Painless: track def-dispatched string concat (#153436)

Charge a def-dispatched '+' that resolves at runtime to a string
concatenation against the per-context allocation limit. The operand
types are unknown at compile time, so the charge is emitted at the def
'+' site (like the statically-typed concat pre-check): spill the
operands, charge the concat estimate, then reload and run the real add.

author: Oleg Lvovitch
message:
ES|QL: relax hive-partition IT node-count assertion for pruning (#153721)

* ES|QL: relax hive-partition IT node-count assertion for pruning

The external scan distributes to fewer nodes once partition pruning
(#153640) reduces the split set, so >= 2 data nodes is no longer
guaranteed. Relax to >= 1; row-count and parity assertions unchanged.

* [CI] Auto commit changes from spotless

---------

Co-authored-by: elasticsearchmachine <infra-root+elasticsearchmachine@elastic.co>

author: Nik Everett
message:
ESQL: Allow expression on LHS of match operator (`:`) (#151890)

Allows expressions like:
```
| WHERE CONCAT("a", field):"something"
```

Previously the `:` operator only allowed a field. Now support expressions. These will only *work* if you set the runtime pragma.

author: Costin Leau
message:
[ESQL] Bypass permits for storage metadata ops (#153686)

Metadata ops (length/lastModified/exists) took the same permit as data streams, which hold it for their whole minutes-long lifetime. Under load this parked metadata HEAD/stat calls behind held stream permits, starving the dedicated esql_external_io discovery/read pool (and stalling the esql_worker drivers waiting on it).

author: Jack Conradson
message:
Painless: allocation annotations for Elasticsearch field-access allocators (#153435)

Annotate the common field-access allocators in the ES base/context
allowlists. Dynamic: BytesRef.utf8ToString (String sized to the ref's
byte length) and ScriptDocValues.GeoPoints.getLats/getLons (double[]
sized to the point count). Constant: GeoPoint (32b), IPAddress (64b),
CIDR (48b), ScriptTermStats.StatsSummary accessors (48b), and the
sha1/sha256/sha512 augmentations (fixed 40/64/128-char hex strings).
